instructions.what_to_build: One complete self-contained single-page website per lead in this manifest (20 leads). Each site is ONE file named <slug>.html with ALL CSS and JS inline. No external fonts, scripts, or stylesheets. These are trial websites for small local trade businesses in Yorkshire, England — they must impress the owner enough to buy.
instructions.quality_bar_CRITICAL: These pages are sold to the business owners, so they must look like a professional agency built them — NOT like a template. Spend equal, full effort on every one of the 20 sites, including the last ones. A flat page of centred text blocks is a failure. Every site needs: a designed hero with visual depth (layered gradients from the palette, a large CSS/SVG decorative motif drawn from the trade — e.g. a roofline silhouette for a roofer, leaf shapes for a gardener, tile grid for a tiler), distinct section backgrounds that alternate rhythm, cards or split layouts for services (not plain bullet lists), generous whitespace, a sticky header with the business name and a call button, large expressive typography (use system font stacks with strong weights/sizes — e.g. 900-weight condensed-feel headings via font-stretch/letter-spacing), hover states, subtle scroll animations (CSS only, respect prefers-reduced-motion), and a strong footer CTA band.
instructions.variety_rule: The 20 sites must NOT share one layout. Vary hero composition (split hero / full-bleed gradient / diagonal section breaks / boxed card hero), section order, and graphic motifs so each looks individually designed for that trade. Two sites in the same trade must still look clearly different.
instructions.hero_logo_CRITICAL: Do NOT fetch or write any base64/data URIs — the real images are injected after delivery. Every site's hero must contain using that literal placeholder token (the lead's real logo pixel dims are provided — size .hlogo via aspect-ratio, max-width: min(320px, 70vw)). If the logo would clash with the hero background, sit it on a light card with padding. Do NOT substitute a text wordmark. Do NOT try to fetch ad_image_url.
instructions.content: Each lead now includes ad_text (verbatim transcription of their printed magazine advert), services[], tagline, and trust_points[] — all taken from the real ad. Build the services section from services[] (one card each, with a short benefit-focused sentence you write for that trade). Use tagline in the hero if present. Use trust_points for a why-choose-us strip. You may write natural marketing copy around these facts (local, friendly, reliable framing) but never invent specific claims: no made-up years of experience, prices, reviews, certifications, or guarantees that are not in trust_points/ad_text.
instructions.design: palette[] is extracted from the business's LOGO. The site's colours MUST visibly match the logo: palette[0] (or the most saturated palette colour) is the primary brand colour for the header, buttons and section accents; the others are supporting tones; derive light/dark tints for backgrounds. A visitor must never see a logo whose colours clash with the page around it. Mobile-first, fast, accessible (contrast AA, focus states). Sections: hero (logo, business name, tagline/one-line pitch, phone CTA with tel: link), services, why-choose-us / trust points, service area (town + surrounding area, name nearby towns), contact.
instructions.trade_photo: Every lead has a professional 16:9 trade photo (dims in photo_width/photo_height) injected after delivery. Use the literal token __PHOTO_DATAURI__ exactly once per site — as the hero background-image (with a palette-tinted overlay for text contrast) or a split-hero/section . Every site must display it.
instructions.contact_form: Every site gets a contact form posting to https://formspree.io/f/mpqgeoaj with method POST, fields: name, phone, message, and a hidden input name='_subject' value='<business> enquiry'. Also show the business phone number prominently with a tel: link.
instructions.phone_rule: Use the exact phone (and landline if present) given in this manifest. Never invent phone numbers, emails, addresses, prices, or reviews.
instructions.email_rule: Only show an email address if the manifest email field is non-empty; use it exactly as written.
instructions.footer: Small footer: '<business> — <town>, <county>'. No fake copyright years, no invented company registration numbers.
instructions.seo: Each page: <title><business> | <category> in <town></title> and a meta description using the ad_text.
instructions.deliverable: Zip all 20 files as batch3.N-sites.zip (N = this manifest's number) and provide a download link or upload URL (litterbox.catbox.moe 72h, or any fetchable URL).
